purpose: Reference documentation for all commands in {tracker_root}/_commands/Synapse/. Covers swarm lifecycle (!p_track, !p_track_plan, !p, !master_plan_track, !dispatch, !retry, !resume, !track_resume, !p_track_resume, !add_task, !eager_dispatch, !export, !cancel, !cancel-safe), monitoring (!status, !logs, !inspect, !deps, !history, !update_dashboard), server control (!start, !stop, !reset), and project management (!project). Includes comparison tables for overlapping resume/dispatch commands.
gotchas: !p_track agents become MASTER agents -- they NEVER write code
!cancel marks in-progress as failed but agents may still run; !cancel-safe waits gracefully
Comparison: !p_track_resume (full lifecycle) vs !track_resume (dispatch-focused) vs !dispatch --ready (pending only) vs !retry (single task)
!add_task only modifies pending tasks -- never completed/in_progress
!eager_dispatch is one-shot dispatch only -- does not monitor, retry, or report
!cancel writing progress files is the ONE exception where master writes them directly
patterns: Three-phase swarm structure: Context/Planning -> Execution -> Report
Hierarchical multi-stream: meta-planner -> child master agents -> worker agents
Dependency-driven dispatch: tasks dispatched the instant deps satisfied
Circuit breaker auto-replan: 3+ failures or blast radius > 50%
conventions: Dashboard arg defaults to assigned via DASHBOARD ID: directive
All commands document Purpose, Syntax, Arguments, Key Behavior, and Produces files
Comparison tables for commands with overlapping scope
